macro_summary: The Fed held rates steady at 3.50%-3.75% at its March 19 meeting — confirming the rate-pause thesis that has been constraining tech multiples for weeks. Markets absorbed the decision with minimal drama: SPY edged down 0.17% to $659.80, VIX compressed 4.11% to 24.06, and the 10-year yield ticked up modestly to 4.28%, reinforcing that cuts remain off the table until inflation resets. The DXY slipped 0.74% to 99.37, a mild dollar reversal that offers slight relief to multinationals but does not change the structural picture. Non-Farm Payrolls on April 3 is now the next major macro catalyst.
commodities_note: WTI fell 3.34% to $93.15 on the session — a notable pullback from recent Hormuz-driven highs but still well above pre-crisis levels, consistent with the spike-and-partial-recovery pattern analysts flagged. Note that intraday data from alternative sources showed WTI near $92.92–$96.57 range; the canonical closing figure of $93.15 is used here. Gold dropped 3.46% to $4,684.70 — a sharp single-session decline from recent highs, likely reflecting profit-taking and a modest dollar bounce. Despite today's gold pullback, the metal remains historically elevated; GLD is extended well above its 50-day average and no attractive entry exists at current levels.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Watch for a sustained move above SGD 57.58 with volume for an upgrade to WATCH.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Becomes actionable on a pullback toward SGD 21.00 (50-day average) with improving volume.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Reassess below SGD 37.12 (50-day average support) or on an upgrade trigger above SGD 37.38 with volume.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — price has slipped below the declining 50-day average and analyst consensus implies a 64.3% expected earnings decline, which is a material concern that must be weighed against any entry. SIA closed at SGD 6.59, 0.7% below its 50-day average of SGD 6.64 with RSI at 44.9 (neutral-bearish zone), and the one-month performance is down 5.59% as the ongoing Hormuz disruption continues to inflate jet fuel costs and compress margins on affected routes. The 64% expected earnings decline is not yet reflected in forward multiples — at 17.3x forward PE, SIA trades at a 24.5% premium to the Singapore cluster median of 13.9x, which is difficult to justify given the earnings trajectory. The 8.32% FCF yield is genuinely attractive and the 5.31% dividend yield provides income support, but with revenue growth at just 1.9% and the Hormuz crisis still active, the cash generation could come under pressure if disruption extends. The 200-day average at SGD 6.55 is the next meaningful support floor, roughly 0.6% below current price — not a lot of cushion. Bottom line: SIA looks cheap on yield and cash flow but the earnings outlook is genuinely alarming given current fuel cost headwinds. Avoid until the Hormuz situation stabilises or the stock pulls back to the SGD 6.34 support zone.

Z74_SI.entry_block: BLOCKED: +7.5% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 63 (>=55).
Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. The hard block remains in force: Singtel is 7.5% above its 50-day average with RSI at 62.8 — the pipeline blocks entry above 5% extension. At 25.6x forward PE versus the cluster median of 13.9x, the 84.2% valuation premium to Singapore peers requires justification the current revenue growth of 0.9% simply cannot provide. The PEG of 3.76 flags this as expensive for the growth on offer. Wait for a pullback toward SGD 4.81 (50-day average) before reassessing.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVDA closed at $178.56, sitting almost precisely on its 200-day average of $178.26 — a meaningful support test after drifting 3.4% below the declining 50-day average of $184.92. RSI at 43.6 is neutral-bearish but not yet oversold. The Blackwell Ultra ramp remains the core thesis: per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call, the Q1 FY2027 guide of $78B implies Blackwell is shipping faster than many modelled, and a confirmed deal to supply 1 million GPUs to Amazon Web Services by end of 2027 (per Reuters, citing a Nvidia executive) adds near-term revenue visibility. Lenovo's expanded NVIDIA AI alliance targeting the FIFA World Cup 2026 adds enterprise and sports infrastructure demand. Valuation is actually compelling: 16.1x forward PE with PEG of 1.09 and 73.2% revenue growth — NVDA is trading below its cluster median PE of 19.1x, the cheapest it has been relative to semis peers in recent memory. The problem is purely technical: the 50-day average is declining, price is below it, and the broader market is not providing the breadth needed to sustain a breakout. The $177.41 support just below current price is the key level — a close below that would open the door toward $169.81. Bottom line: NVDA is arguably the cheapest high-quality name on the watchlist relative to its growth rate, but the declining 50-day average and fragile market breadth argue for patience. Wait for a confirmed reclaim of $184-185 with volume before entering.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. AMD bounced 2.97% to $205.27 but remains 4.4% below a declining 50-day average of $214.62, keeping the signal in CAUTION territory. The fundamental picture is arguably the most attractive on the watchlist: at 19.1x forward PE with a PEG of 0.59 and 34.1% revenue growth, AMD is at fair value relative to its semis cluster peers — the growth is running well ahead of the multiple. The confirmed Meta GPU supply deal per AMD's IR press release (February 24, 2026) — a multi-year partnership for MI450-based accelerators with up to 160 million share warrants tied to shipment milestones — validates AMD as a credible NVIDIA alternative at hyperscale scale. Samsung's capital expenditure plan for AI semiconductors including HBM4 and DDR5 memory supply for AMD accelerators (per Simply Wall St., March 19) adds supply-chain depth to the thesis. FCF yield at 1.37% is thin and worth noting — the thesis is growth-dependent, not cash-generation-dependent today. The $218.84 resistance is the first meaningful ceiling above; below, the $205 area needs to hold or the next support cluster is far down at $149-158. Bottom line: AMD is the best-valued growth name on the watchlist — cheap for what you're getting. Wait for the price to reclaim and hold above the 50-day average around $214 on volume before entering; the setup improves materially on that confirmation.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 47x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — Intel has edged back toward its 50-day average of $46.51, now just 0.71% below it with RSI recovering to 51.5 (neutral-bullish), which removes the acute technical deterioration that justified the CAUTION designation. The pipeline's soft block (forward PE of 47x) still applies and warrants explicit justification: Intel's foundry thesis centres on improving 18A node yields, with the company confirming steady yield improvements over the past 7-8 months (per Overclock3D, citing Intel's John Pitzer), and a 14A roadmap with more mature PDK. The forward/trailing PE divergence (46.6x vs. no trailing PE available due to negative earnings) signals an expected earnings inflection rather than current profitability — this is a turnaround story priced for recovery, not for today's cash flows. Negative FCF yield of -1.95% and revenue declining 4.1% YoY mean the thesis depends entirely on the foundry ramp materialising. INTC earnings April 24 will be the first real data point on whether 18A yield improvements are translating to external customer wins and improved margins. The $46.97 resistance cited in prior reports is the immediate ceiling; below, $42.58 is the key support. No entry recommended given valuation and negative FCF — this is a watch-and-verify situation into April earnings.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — ASML has slipped back to 1.3% below its 50-day average of $1,384.59 after yesterday's signal upgrade, and the broader market's failure to build on relief-rally momentum removes the technical justification for WATCH. RSI at 47.8 is neutral-bearish. The imec High-NA EUV tool acquisition (per Reuters, March 18) — one of fewer than a dozen such tools globally, purchased for approximately $400 million — confirms that the next-generation chipmaking pipeline is advancing, with Intel and SK Hynix preparing to use these tools for AI logic and HBM chips as early as 2027. Per ASML's Q4 2025 earnings call, the company projects revenue reaching $71B by 2030 on strong EUV demand. However, at 31.8x forward PE with a PEG of 2.26 and revenue growth of only 4.9%, ASML is 66.5% more expensive than the semis cluster median — expensive for the near-term growth rate, even if the 2030 thesis is sound. ASML earnings on April 15 is the next catalyst that could flip this signal; a strong order book update would be the trigger to upgrade toward WATCH. Support at $1,366 is being tested today; $1,407 is the first resistance above.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +11.4% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=9 (<30), RSI 58 (>=55).
MU.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — Micron's blowout Q2 FY2026 earnings (record revenue, HBM datacenter demand confirmed per the March 18, 2026 earnings release) have fundamentally changed the picture, and the stock's 11.4% extension above the 50-day average reflects that repricing. The pipeline hard block prevents a BUY or WATCH signal at this extension, and rightly so — entering 11% extended into a market with VIX at 24 is poor risk management. Valuation is striking: 4.7x forward PE versus a cluster median of 19.1x (75% discount), with 56.7% revenue growth and analyst consensus expecting 175% earnings growth. The PEG of 0.60 is genuine — this is not a value trap but a cyclical memory name repricing as HBM demand tightens supply into 2027. However, the FCF yield of 0.09% is essentially zero, and the trailing PE of 42.2x shows the earnings inflection is still in progress. The $455.50 resistance is the next ceiling; below, $363-357 is the meaningful support cluster. No entry at current levels — wait for either a pullback toward $400-410 (the rising 50-day average) or confirmation that the $455 resistance breaks with volume.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon trades at $208.76, 5.79% below a declining 50-day average of $221.59, with RSI at 44.1 in neutral-bearish territory. The $200B AI capex commitment (per Amazon's Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K filing) remains the defining tension: massive investment that pressures near-term operating income but positions AWS for structural cloud and AI leadership. Per Bloomberg (March 20), Amazon has confirmed it will pour more than $100 billion into capital expenditures this year including AI infrastructure in India. The confirmed Nvidia deal — 1 million GPUs to AWS by end of 2027, with deliveries starting this year per Reuters (March 19, citing a Nvidia executive) — adds GPU supply certainty but also validates the capex intensity. At 22.3x forward PE with PEG of 1.96, valuation is in line with BigTech peers — not expensive, but FCF yield of 1.06% means the thesis is entirely forward-looking. With earnings only 5% expected to grow and price below a declining 50-day average, there's no technical entry argument today. The $196.93 support is the key level to watch; a break below opens significant downside toward $161. Reassess if price reclaims $219-221 with volume.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Alphabet is 3.96% below its declining 50-day average at $305.73 with RSI at 46.1. The Pentagon AI contract expansion (per GuruFocus, March 19) adds government revenue diversification, and the Health100 platform with CVS Health (per Simply Wall St., March 19) extends Gemini into consumer healthcare — both incrementally positive for the Cloud segment. Per Bloomberg (March 20), Alphabet has committed more than $100 billion in capex this year including AI infrastructure. The PEG of 2.30 flags this as expensive for the growth rate despite 18% revenue growth; FCF yield of 1.03% means the stock is almost entirely valued on future earnings. At 22.8x forward PE, GOOG is exactly at the BigTech cluster median — fairly valued for now, but the declining 50-day average means the trend is not supportive. GOOG earnings April 24 is the next catalyst. The $295.95 support zone is the level to watch below; $311 is the first meaningful resistance. No entry until the 50-day average stabilises and price reclaims it.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +45.1% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.
LITE.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 31% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — at 45.1% above the 50-day average with RSI at 65.4, the extreme extension is now incompatible with a HOLD designation and the pipeline hard block is clear. Lumentum surged another 9.53% today to $772.13, extending a 25% five-day move on Bank of America price target upgrades (per Investopedia, March 19) and sustained enthusiasm around its optical interconnect and co-packaged optics (CPO) positioning for AI datacenters. The confirmed NVIDIA $2 billion strategic investment per NVIDIA's investor relations press release (March 2, 2026) remains the structural foundation. However, valuation is stretched: at 52.9x forward PE, negative FCF yield of -0.04%, and a PEG of 0.16 that appears anomalous given the forward PE — this PEG should be verified against the earnings growth estimate before citing as a valuation argument. The 50-day average is 31% below current price — a crash-level pullback would be required to reach it. Use the $548.24 shorter-term support as the relevant reference. No entry at current levels — this needs a material pullback before risk/reward improves.

COHR.entry_block: BLOCKED: +20.9% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.
COHR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Coherent gained 7.31% to $275.57 on Stifel price target increases (per StockStory, March 18) and Bank of America analyst upgrades (per Investopedia, March 19), but the stock is now 20.91% above its 50-day average — the extreme extension threshold — triggering the hard block. RSI at 60.5 is not overbought but the price extension alone disqualifies entry. The NVIDIA $2 billion strategic investment in silicon photonics (per NVIDIA's newsroom press release, March 2, 2026) is confirmed and structural. The PEG of 0.06 is anomalous — with a 37.3x forward PE, this implies an earnings growth estimate that appears inconsistent; treat it as unverified rather than a valuation argument. FCF yield of 0.25% is near-zero. The $230 support (near the 50-day average) is the relevant entry zone — approximately 17% below current price. No entry until a meaningful pullback occurs toward that level.

XLE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +12.2% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 76 (>=55).
XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. XLE gained 1.42% to $59.36 despite WTI pulling back 3.34% — the energy sector ETF is now 12.2% above its 50-day average with RSI at 76.1, deep in overbought territory. The pipeline hard block is unambiguous. The Hormuz disruption continues to provide a geopolitical supply premium, but WTI's 3.34% pullback today signals the spike-and-partial-recovery pattern analysts had flagged, and at RSI 76 the risk/reward is clearly unfavourable for fresh entry. The geopolitical driver has not graduated to structural: it remains a discrete military conflict event, not a multi-quarter fundamental re-rating of energy sector earnings. No entry — if WTI were to sustain above $100 for 60+ days and producer free cash flows were visibly revised upward, the thesis could strengthen. Current XLE equivalent entry would require a pullback toward $53-54 (near the 50-day average), representing roughly a 10% decline from current levels.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 83x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — Palantir has reclaimed its 50-day average of $152.19 and held above it for 3 sessions, with price at $155.68 (2.29% above) and RSI at 57.4 in neutral-bullish territory, qualifying for WATCH. The defense AI spending surge from the Iran conflict is the primary catalyst: the confirmed $448 million Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) and the $10 billion Army IDIQ framework ceiling (per government contract databases — note this is a framework ceiling, not firm revenue) represent meaningful near-term revenue. Mizuho reiterated Outperform with a $195 price target after an executive meeting (per GuruFocus, March 19). The pipeline soft block (83.4x forward PE) requires explicit justification: 70% revenue growth with analyst consensus expecting 647.6% earnings growth — that earnings expansion trajectory justifies the premium multiple for a high-conviction defense AI thesis. PEG of 3.02 flags the valuation as expensive on a normalised growth basis, and FCF yield of 0.34% is effectively zero. The 265.8% premium to BigTech cluster peers is the key risk. Volume is currently low — a meaningful entry requires volume confirmation. Entry zone $152-156 with a stop below $149; PLTR earnings May 5 is the forward catalyst. Bottom line: PLTR is expensive, but defense AI spending driven by the Iran conflict could sustain this multiple if revenue keeps compounding at 70%. Watch for volume to confirm before entering.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Would reassess above the 50-day average near $132 with sustained volume and VIX settling below 22.

singapore.summary: The cluster remains in mixed HOLD/CAUTION territory with no reset signal. The key development is C6L_SI's signal downgrade to CAUTION — the 64% expected earnings decline and Hormuz-driven fuel cost pressure make it the cluster's most acute risk. NIM compression from the confirmed Fed rate pause continues to weigh on the banking trio (DBS, OCBC, UOB), while Singtel's 84% valuation premium to cluster peers remains unjustified by 0.9% revenue growth.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — rate pause and Hormuz headwinds unresolved
singapore.key_development: C6L_SI downgraded to CAUTION as 64% expected earnings decline and Hormuz fuel cost inflation create a fundamentally deteriorating setup that the 5.3% yield cannot offset.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX gained 1.22% to $340.20 — outperforming SPY materially — confirming the mid-cap foundry rotation thesis. MU upgraded to HOLD after blowout earnings while ASML slipped back to CAUTION as the technical 50-day retest failed to hold. The cluster is deeply bifurcated: MU and the foundry thesis are alive, while NVDA and AMD remain technically blocked despite genuinely compelling valuations well below the SOXX benchmark's implied multiples.
semiconductors.key_development: ASML's imec High-NA EUV tool acquisition (per Reuters) confirms the next-generation chipmaking pipeline is advancing toward 2027 commercial deployment, but the stock's failure to sustain the 50-day average reclaim shifts it back to CAUTION.

big_tech.summary: PLTR's upgrade to WATCH is the one bright spot — defense AI contract momentum is sufficient to justify monitoring despite extreme valuation. AMZN and GOOG remain in CAUTION, both below declining 50-day averages, with confirmed $100B+ capex commitments per Bloomberg adding near-term earnings pressure. The cluster's defining tension remains unresolved: structural AI demand is real, but rate-sensitive multiples under a sustained Fed pause compress the entry case.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautious — entry blocked on AMZN/GOOG; PLTR sole actionable name
big_tech.key_development: PLTR reclaimed its 50-day average and held for 3 sessions — the first confirmed technical entry signal in the BigTech cluster since the market pullback began.

photonics.summary: Both LITE and COHR hit extreme extension thresholds today — LITE at 45% above its 50-day average and COHR at 21% — triggering hard blocks on both. Bank of America target upgrades (per Investopedia) and sustained AI optics enthusiasm are real, but the S&P 500 inclusion buying thesis has long been exhausted and these moves are running purely on sentiment.
photonics.thesis_status: Hard-blocked — extreme extension on both names
photonics.key_development: LITE surged another 9.53% today bringing the five-day move to 25%, but with negative FCF yield and a 45% extension, risk/reward has inverted — the confirmed NVIDIA investment thesis is intact but the price has run far ahead of it.

energy.summary: XLE advanced 1.42% despite WTI pulling back 3.34% — a divergence that suggests energy equities are pricing in a more sustained supply disruption than today's commodity move implies. RSI at 76.1 is deeply overbought and the 12.2% extension above the 50-day average makes this a tactical avoid regardless of the Hormuz narrative.
energy.thesis_status: Overbought — geopolitical premium priced, no entry
energy.key_development: WTI's 3.34% pullback to $93.15 suggests the spike-and-partial-recovery pattern is playing out as analysts predicted, but XLE's resilience indicates the market is not yet pricing in full demand destruction.

index.summary: VUAA_L remains on CAUTION at $126.80, 3.96% below a declining 50-day average with RSI at 35.4 — approaching oversold territory. The confirmed Fed rate pause removes the near-term catalyst for a 50-day average reclaim, and VIX at 24 keeps the risk environment elevated.
index.thesis_status: Cautious — below declining 50-day average, no entry trigger
index.key_development: The Fed's confirmed rate hold at 3.50%-3.75% removes any near-term dovish catalyst, cementing the view that VUAA_L's 50-day average recovery requires either a VIX compression toward 20 or a meaningful inflation surprise.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM)
interconnected.reason: Primary manufacturer of NVDA Blackwell and AMD MI450 chips — both watchlist names have confirmed AI GPU ramps that run directly through TSMC fabs at high utilisation.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels, or revisit after NVDA and AMD report Q1 earnings to confirm the Blackwell and MI450 ramp pacing.

interconnected.name: Broadcom (AVGO)
interconnected.reason: Custom AI chip supplier to Google (TPU v7) and Meta — both GOOG and AMZN's capex cycles drive Broadcom networking and ASIC revenue directly.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes more interesting if GOOG reclaims its 50-day average on Q1 earnings (April 24), confirming TPU demand is translating to Broadcom orders.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: Dominant HBM3e/HBM4 supplier to NVDA and ASML's confirmed High-NA EUV customer — directly connected to both the memory tightening thesis and the ASML node ramp timeline.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after MU Q3 earnings (approximate June 2026) for confirmation that HBM pricing and supply tightness are sustaining — SK Hynix benefits from the same demand cycle.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz conflict (Day 11): commercial maritime traffic effectively halted at 2 vessels/day versus 138 baseline; Iran targeting Gulf neighbour tankers while continuing own exports via secondary channels
US-China technology war: 100% tariffs on China goods, advanced chip export bans reshaping semiconductor supply chains; China PMI contracted for second consecutive month
Fed rate pause extending: inflation sticky at 0.3% MoM CPI; confirmed hold at 3.50%-3.75% with no cut signal; US10Y at 4.28%
US military escalation in Latin America: Venezuela escalation adding tail risk to broader geopolitical volatility backdrop
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz crisis keeps XLE overbought at RSI 76 and has tipped C6L_SI into CAUTION on fuel cost margin risk — the two most direct first-order impacts on the watchlist. The Fed's confirmed rate pause is suppressing AMZN and GOOG below declining 50-day averages and keeping VUAA_L in CAUTION, as elevated duration costs compress multiples on capex-heavy tech names.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Fed confirmed rate hold at 3.50%-3.75% on March 19 — removes any near-term dovish catalyst and solidifies the rate-pause thesis as the dominant market headwind.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash, no new entries on extended names; selectively watch PLTR (defense AI beneficiary) and NVDA (at 200-day support) for confirmation signals before committing capital.

events_this_week.event: Next trading session opens with WTI -3.34% and Gold -3.46% — commodity volatility elevated
Non-Farm Payrolls (March) — first major macro print post-Fed rate hold
ASML Q1 2026 earnings — order book update could resolve CAUTION signal; High-NA EUV demand the key metric
Intel Q1 2026 earnings — first data point on 18A yield improvements translating to external customer wins and margin recovery
Alphabet (GOOG) Q1 2026 earnings — TPU monetisation and Cloud growth rate are the signal-change catalysts
Palantir Q1 2026 earnings — defense contract revenue conversion from Army/Navy deals is the thesis test for the WATCH signal
AMD Q1 2026 earnings — MI450 ramp pacing and Meta deal first shipment confirmation

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — broadly cautious with two isolated watch opportunities
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The confirmed Fed rate pause at 3.50%-3.75% is the single most important risk factor: it caps tech multiples, keeps AMZN/GOOG/VUAA_L below declining 50-day averages, and means any recovery in the watchlist requires either an inflation surprise or earnings beats that justify current valuations without rate support.

watch_for_entry.note: Reclaimed 50-day average, holding for 3 sessions. Defense AI contract momentum supports the thesis. Wait for volume confirmation before entering.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter $152-156 on volume confirmation, stop below $149

watch_for_entry.note: 2.25% above rising 50-day average with low volume — needs a pullback to the average for a clean entry.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter SGD 21.00-21.10 on pullback to 50-day average, stop below SGD 20.50

on_deck.note: At 200-day average support. 16.1x forward PE with 73% revenue growth — cheapest quality name on the watchlist relative to growth.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 1.09, 73.2% revenue growth, trading below semis cluster median PE — CAUTION is purely technical, not fundamental.
on_deck.trigger: Price reclaims and holds $184-185 (50-day average) with above-average volume on a session where SPY is also positive.

on_deck.note: PEG 0.59 with 34% revenue growth — best growth-adjusted value on the watchlist. Technically blocked below declining 50-day average.
on_deck.rationale: Confirmed Meta GPU supply deal (AMD IR press release, Feb 24, 2026) validates hyperscale positioning; at peer median PE with superior growth.
on_deck.trigger: Price reclaims $214-215 (50-day average) on volume, or a broad semis-positive catalyst (e.g. strong ASML April earnings).

caution_trim.note: Avoid. 64% expected earnings decline with Hormuz fuel cost pressure and a 24.5% premium to Singapore cluster peers — risk/reward is unfavourable.
Avoid. 84% premium to Singapore cluster median with 0.9% revenue growth and PEG of 3.76 — hard-blocked 7.5% above 50-day average.
Avoid at current levels. 66.5% premium to semis peers with 4.9% revenue growth; failed to hold 50-day average reclaim. Revisit after April 15 earnings.
Avoid. Below declining 50-day average, $200B capex pressuring near-term margins, FCF yield 1.06% — entry unattractive until above $219.
Avoid. PEG 2.30 expensive for 18% growth, below declining 50-day average, FCF yield 1.03%. Wait for April 24 earnings.
Avoid. 45% above 50-day average, negative FCF yield, extreme extension — hard-blocked regardless of NVIDIA investment thesis.
Avoid. 21% above 50-day average triggers extreme extension hard block. Wait for pullback toward $230 support.
Avoid. RSI 76.1 overbought, 12.2% above 50-day average — geopolitical premium is priced. No entry until pullback toward $53-54.
Avoid. Below declining 50-day average with Fed rate pause removing near-term dovish catalyst. Reassess above $132 with VIX below 22.
Avoid current levels. Blowout earnings are priced — 11.4% above 50-day average hard-blocks entry. Wait for pullback toward $400-410.

hold_no_action.note: Upgrades to WATCH on sustained move above SGD 57.58 with volume. Reassess below SGD 56.50.
Upgrades to WATCH if price reclaims SGD 37.38 (50-day average) with volume. Reassess below SGD 36.80.
No entry given negative FCF and 47x forward PE. Upgrades to WATCH on April 24 earnings if 18A customer wins are confirmed. Reassess below $42.58 support.

new_stocks_to_watch.note: NVDA and AMD GPU ramps run through TSMC fabs — direct beneficiary of Blackwell Ultra and MI450 volume production.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Add to watchlist on 8-10% pullback from current levels, or after NVDA/AMD Q1 earnings confirm ramp pacing

new_stocks_to_watch.note: Custom AI chip (TPU v7) supplier to GOOG and networking beneficiary of AMZN's $200B capex cycle.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Revisit after GOOG April 24 earnings confirms TPU demand trajectory; watch for pullback toward 50-day average

new_stocks_to_watch.note: SK Hynix is the dominant HBM supplier to NVDA — MU's blowout earnings confirm the memory tightening cycle benefits both names.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Revisit after MU Q3 earnings (approx. June 2026) for HBM pricing sustainability confirmation; prefer US-listed equivalent if available

contrarian_candidates.key_metric: Broad S&P 500 ETF; RSI approaching oversold threshold
contrarian_candidates.watching_for: VIX drops below 22 on two consecutive sessions AND SPY reclaims its 50-day average with above-average volume — both conditions required before considering a starter position.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Payrolls come in below 150K — labour market softening gives Fed cover to signal a rate cut timeline, compressing the 10-year yield back below 4.20%
bullish_upgrades: AMZN → WATCH (50-day average reclaim becomes feasible)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Payrolls above 250K — strong labour market entrenches rate pause, US10Y pushes above 4.40%, dollar strengthens
bearish_impact: AMZN remains CAUTION — multiple compression accelerates
Singapore banks face further NIM compression pressure
VUAA_L risks breaking 200-day average support at $127.06

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Strong EUV order book growth, High-NA customer ramp confirmation, revenue guidance above consensus
bullish_upgrades: ASML → WATCH (50-day average reclaim with fundamental confirmation)
NVDA → on_deck conviction increases (foundry demand validates AI capex)
MU → entry window opens on order pipeline read-through
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Order book softness, China restrictions impacting backlog, guidance miss on margin
